claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by gathering some basic information:

Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children. I'm asking about both of them - Alex, who's 9, and Blake, who's 5. They both live at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! To help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex and Blake, I need to understand the eligibility rules first. Let me review the official guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I now understand the eligibility rules. Since both Alex (9) and Blake (5) are under 16 and live at home with you, I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

Are you currently living in the UK?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

Are either Alex or Blake currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not. They're both at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question:

Are you responsible for Alex and Blake - meaning you're caring for them and providing for their needs such as food, clothes, and accommodation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I take care of both of them - I provide everything they need, their food, clothes, and of course they live here with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me and the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now assess your eligibility for both Alex and Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's why:
- **Alex (9 years old)**: You meet all the eligibility requirements - Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their care, and no one else is claiming for them.
- **Blake (5 years old)**: You also meet all the eligibility requirements - Blake is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for their care, and no one else is claiming for them.

You can claim Child Benefit for both children. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK (Manchester), you are responsible for their care and provide for their needs (food, clothes, accommodation), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in hospital or care. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

Blake is 5 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK (Manchester), you are responsible for their care and provide for their needs (food, clothes, accommodation), no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake, and Blake is not in hospital or care. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children, Alex (9) and Blake (5), both under 16 years old. The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with both children, is responsible for their care and provides for all their needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. Neither child is in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for both children.
